output.title: Landlord Withholding Security Deposit? Your 3-Step Recovery Plan
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn what deductions are illegal, how to write a demand letter, and recover your money fast.
content_outline: Introduction: The Real Cost of a Withheld Security Deposit
Know Your Rights: What a Landlord Can and Cannot Deduct
Top Reasons Landlords Wrongfully Keep Deposits (And Why They’re Illegal)
What to Do When Your Landlord Won't Return Your Deposit: A Step-by-Step Guide
High-Converting Email Subject Lines & Body Copy for Your Demand Letter
How to Escalate: Small Claims Court, Statutory Penalties, and Legal Pressure
Conclusion: Take Back Your Money Without Hiring a Lawyer
key_points: Email Subject Line 1: 'Formal Demand: Immediate Return of Security Deposit — [Property Address]'
Email Subject Line 2: 'Urgent: Security Deposit Overdue Under [State] Law — Action Required'
Email Subject Line 3: 'Legal Notice: Failure to Return Security Deposit by [Deadline]'
Body Copy Opening: State move-out date, deposit amount, and the exact state-mandated deadline your landlord missed to establish legal urgency.
Body Copy Body: Cite your state’s security deposit statute, itemize unlawful deductions (normal wear and tear, pre-existing damage), and demand full payment within 7 days.
Body Copy Closing: Warn of escalation to small claims court, statutory penalties, and interest; attach move-in/move-out photo evidence and certified mail tracking.
Fact: Most states require deposit return within 14–60 days; deductions must be itemized in writing. Normal wear and tear is never a valid deduction.
Psychological Trigger: Tenants who send a formal, state-specific demand letter recover deposits 3x faster than those who rely on text messages or phone calls.
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate a state-specific security deposit demand letter with DepositBack in under 5 minutes and start your recovery today.
output.target_audience: Problem-aware US renters whose landlords are wrongfully withholding, failing to return, or making illegal deductions from security deposits; actively searching for demand letter templates and legal next steps.
